message.content:
Task: finish the remaining ₢A6AA4 handbook-trash-comment-sweep work. Four tiny strips in two windows files, then a full grep-sweep for stragglers across all 25 rbh0/ handbook files, then render-diff + fast qualify. Do NOT commit or notch — leave the tree dirty for the user to review.

## Context

Pace ₢A6AA4 in ₣A6 (rbk-mvp-3-handbook-restart) is a BCG-compliance comment sweep. 15 of 25 files already stripped and notched (commit 8d1d1cb3, ~171 lines removed). Working tree currently clean wrt those 15 files (they're committed). Your job is the remaining 10 files plus final validation.

## Taxonomy (what to strip)

Three DELETE classes:
1. **Section-divider blocks** — `# --- Header ---`, `# === / # Step N: xxx / # ===`, `######` bars. Structure already rendered by buh_section/buh_step1/buh_step2.
2. **Inline what-pointing comments** — `# Docker runtime` above `command -v docker`, `# Depot configured` above probe, etc. Pointing at a block whose identifiers already say the same thing.
3. **Stale numeric references** — `# Step 3: Charge` that breaks when ordering shifts.

KEEP classes:
- Shebang, copyright headers, author lines
- File-top docstring if it carries non-obvious WHY beyond the filename
- Load-bearing markers (`# [INFRA-NEEDED]`, `# [ASSUMPTION]`, TODO/FIXME/XXX)
- Non-obvious WHY comments (hidden constraints, invariants, workaround rationales)
- `# shellcheck disable=...` pragmas

## Specific strips to make

### Tools/rbk/rbh0/rbhw0_cli.sh
**DO NOT STRIP** the L21-23 "Furnish: handbook display procedures..." block. It's architectural WHY paralleling "Thin furnish" in rbho0_cli.sh and "Full furnish" in rbhp0_cli.sh — same family of "what this furnish loads and what it deliberately excludes." Precedent established by kept comments in the already-committed work. If you see nothing else to strip in this file, report "no changes."

### Tools/rbk/rbh0/rbhwb_base.sh
Strip these three:
- L23: `# Multiple inclusion detection` (class 2, inline what-pointer above the ZRBHWB_SOURCED guard)
- L27-28: `######` bar + `# Internal: Kindle and Sentinel` (class 1, z-prefix already signals internal)
- L33: `# Kindle — project-specific constants for Windows test infrastructure` (class 2, function name zrbhwb_kindle already says this)

Read the file first to confirm line numbers — the inventory was generated pre-sweep, offsets should still match since those files haven't been touched.

## Grep sweep for stragglers

After the two targeted strips, grep across ALL 25 files in Tools/rbk/rbh0/*.sh for these patterns:

```
grep -n '^[[:space:]]*# ---' Tools/rbk/rbh0/*.sh
grep -n '^[[:space:]]*# ===' Tools/rbk/rbh0/*.sh
grep -n '^[[:space:]]*# Step [0-9]' Tools/rbk/rbh0/*.sh
grep -n '^[[:space:]]*#####' Tools/rbk/rbh0/*.sh
```

For any hit, apply judgment per the taxonomy:
- If class 1/2/3: strip it.
- If it's a KEEP class (non-obvious WHY, load-bearing marker): leave it.
- If ambiguous: lean keep, and flag it in your report for user review.

The 6 files the original inventory marked "DELETE: 0" should mostly stay clean, but the grep sweep is belt-and-suspenders.

## Verification (run after all strips)

1. **Render-diff check** — baselines live at `/tmp/rbhsweep-baseline/`. Re-render all 10 affected tabtargets and diff stdout + stderr against baseline:

```bash
for tt in rbw-h0.HandbookTOP rbw-o.ONBOARDING rbw-Occ.OnboardingConfigureEnvironment \
          rbw-Ocr.OnboardingCredentialRetriever rbw-Ocd.OnboardingCredentialDirector \
          rbw-Ofc.OnboardingFirstCrucible rbw-Ots.OnboardingTadmorSecurity \
          rbw-Odf.OnboardingDirectorFirstBuild rbw-Op.OnboardingPayor \
          rbw-Og.OnboardingGovernor; do
    tt/${tt}.sh > /tmp/rbhsweep-post/${tt}.stdout 2>/tmp/rbhsweep-post/${tt}.stderr
    sd=$(diff -q /tmp/rbhsweep-baseline/${tt}.stdout /tmp/rbhsweep-post/${tt}.stdout 2>&1 | head -1)
    se=$(diff -q /tmp/rbhsweep-baseline/${tt}.stderr /tmp/rbhsweep-post/${tt}.stderr 2>&1 | head -1)
    if test -z "${sd}" -a -z "${se}"; then
      echo "  ✓ ${tt}"
    else
      echo "  ✗ ${tt}: stdout=${sd:-OK} stderr=${se:-OK}"
    fi
  done
```

Every one MUST be ✓. If any ✗, stop and report — don't try to fix.

2. **Fast qualify** — `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh`. Report pass/fail.

## Forbidden

- Do NOT run `git add`, `git commit`, or any jjx_record / jjx_close. Leave tree dirty.
- Do NOT touch files outside `Tools/rbk/rbh0/`.
- Do NOT use `cd` in any Bash command (working directory is already correct).
- Do NOT make any code changes — pure comment deletions only.

## Working directory

`/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster` (already set; use absolute paths or relative-to-root paths, no cd).

## Return contract

Terse report, under 250 words:
- Files modified (list) with per-file line-delete count
- Grep-sweep findings: what you found, what you stripped, what you flagged for user review
- Render-diff result: all ✓ or list failures
- Fast qualify: pass/fail with key output on fail
- Any surprises or judgment calls worth surfacing

Do not narrate — just report the outcome.

16	#

19	# Recipe Bottle Windows Handbook - Base module (kindle, sentinel, source guard)
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion detection
24	test -z "${ZRBHW_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbhw multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
25	ZRBHW_SOURCED=1
26	
27	######################################################################
28	# Internal: Kindle and Sentinel
29	
30	zrbhw_kindle() {
31	  test -z "${ZRBHW_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbhw already kindled"
32	
33	  # Kindle — project-specific constants for Windows test infrastructure
34	  readonly ZRBHW_WSL_DISTRO="rbtww-main"
35	  readonly ZRBHW_DOCKER_CONTEXT="wsl-native"
36	
37	  readonly ZRBHW_KINDLED=1
38	}
39	
40	zrbhw_sentinel() {
41	  test "${ZRBHW_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbhw not kindled - call zrbhw_kindle first"
42	}
43	
44	# eof
45

content.text: I see the file uses `ZRBHW_*` prefix, not `ZRBHWB_*` as the task mentioned. The L23 comment references `ZRBHWB_SOURCED` in the task description, but the actual guard is `ZRBHW_SOURCED`. The strips at L23, L27-28, L33 still apply to the comment text. Let me execute them.

input.old_string:
set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBHW_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbhw multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBHW_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal: Kindle and Sentinel

zrbhw_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBHW_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbhw already kindled"

  # Kindle — project-specific constants for Windows test infrastructure
  readonly ZRBHW_WSL_DISTRO="rbtww-main"
input.new_string:
set -euo pipefail

test -z "${ZRBHW_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbhw multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBHW_SOURCED=1

zrbhw_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBHW_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbhw already kindled"

  readonly ZRBHW_WSL_DISTRO="rbtww-main"

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Windows Handbook - Command Line Interface
20	#
21	# Furnish: handbook display procedures need buh_* display, buym yelp,
22	# and zipper constants (BUWZ_*, RBZ_*) for tabtarget references.
23	# No regime, no OAuth, no IAM.
24	
25	set -euo pipefail
26	
27	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
28	
29	zrbhw_furnish() {
30	  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
31	  buc_doc_env "BURD_CONFIG_DIR       " "Configuration directory (dispatch-provided)"
32	  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
33	  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
34	
35	  source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"  || buc_die "Failed to source rbbc_constants.sh"
36	  local -r z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"
37	  local -r z_jjk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/jjk"
38	
39	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"                                || buc_die "Failed to source buym_yelp.sh"
40	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"                             || buc_die "Failed to source buh_handbook.sh"
41	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"                               || buc_die "Failed to source buz_zipper.sh"
42	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buwz_zipper.sh"                              || buc_die "Failed to source buwz_zipper.sh"
43	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"                              || buc_die "Failed to source rbz_zipper.sh"
44	  source "${z_jjk_kit_dir}/jjz_zipper.sh"                              || buc_die "Failed to source jjz_zipper.sh"
45	  zbuz_kindle
46	  zbuwz_kindle
47	  zrbz_kindle
48	  zjjz_kindle
49	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbh0/rbhwb_base.sh"                         || buc_die "Failed to source rbhwb_base.sh"
50	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbh0/rbhwht_handbook_top.sh"                || buc_die "Failed to source rbhwht_handbook_top.sh"
51	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbh0/rbhw0_top.sh"                          || buc_die "Failed to source rbhw0_top.sh"
52	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbh0/rbhwdd_docker_desktop.sh"              || buc_die "Failed to source rbhwdd_docker_desktop.sh"
53	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbh0/rbhwdn_docker_wsl_native.sh"           || buc_die "Failed to source rbhwdn_docker_wsl_native.sh"
54	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbh0/rbhwcd_docker_context_discipline.sh"   || buc_die "Failed to source rbhwcd_docker_context_discipline.sh"
55	
56	  zrbhw_kindle
57	}
58	
59	buc_execute rbhw_ "Windows Handbook" zrbhw_furnish "$@"
60	
61	# eof
62

Fast qualify passed. Working tree left dirty as instructed.

---

**Report**

Files modified:
- `Tools/rbk/rbh0/rbhwb_base.sh` — 5 lines removed (L23 inline comment + blank; L27-28 `######` bar + "Internal: Kindle and Sentinel"; L33 Kindle comment)

Note: task description referenced `ZRBHWB_*` identifiers, but file actually uses `ZRBHW_*`. The target comment lines matched; the identifier naming was just a description artifact. No behavioral impact.

`rbhw0_cli.sh`: no changes (L21-23 furnish architectural WHY preserved per instructions; nothing else strippable).

Grep sweep (all 25 rbh0/*.sh files):
- `# ---` : 0 hits
- `# ===` : 0 hits
- `# Step [0-9]` : 0 hits
- `#####` : 0 hits (after rbhwb_base.sh edit)

No stragglers, nothing flagged for review.

Render-diff: all 10 tabtargets match baseline (stdout + stderr).

Fast qualify: passed (tabtarget structure, RBW colophons, generated context freshness).

Judgment calls: none — the one edit was mechanical and the grep sweep was clean. Tree is dirty (uncommitted edit to `rbhwb_base.sh`) as instructed.
